description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic vase with a rounded body and a single, thick handle extending from the side. The surface is adorned with intricate black-figure artwork depicting human figures, possibly mythological or ceremonial scenes, set against a reddish-brown background. The rim and base are decorated with geometric patterns, and the artwork wraps around the entire circumference of the vase. The mouth of the vase is wide, but the rim appears damaged or incomplete, with a large central opening visible from above. The handle is robust and shows signs of wear, while the painted figures remain detailed and expressive. The overall appearance suggests a historical or archaeological artifact, likely intended for display or ceremonial use.
description: A large, round ceramic vase with a single handle and detailed black-figure artwork depicting human scenes on a reddish-brown background. The rim is damaged, and the vase features geometric and figural decorations.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thick handle and a portion of the black-figure artwork, with the rounded body and geometric border visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the wide, damaged mouth and the central opening, with the artwork and figures encircling the rim.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting more of the painted figures and the continuation of the geometric border, with the handle partially visible.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the base and the underside of the handle, with the circular opening and surrounding artwork clearly seen.
